description: KSAE Q&A 게시판의 전체 게시글/답변을 크롤링하여 BGE-M3로 임베딩 후 Qdrant에 업로드하는 RAG 데이터 파이프라인

userStories.title: 프로젝트 구조 및 의존성 설정
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project directory structure and dependencies set up so that subsequent stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory structure: src/, data/raw/, data/processed/
Create requirements.txt with: requests, beautifulsoup4, lxml, FlagEmbedding, numpy, qdrant-client, tqdm, click
Create empty __init__.py in src/
Create placeholder modules: src/crawler.py, src/chunker.py, src/embedder.py, src/uploader.py
Each placeholder module has a docstring describing its purpose
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: 게시판 목록 페이지 크롤링
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to crawl all list pages of the KSAE Q&A board to collect post metadata (id, category, title, author, views, date, detail URL).
acceptanceCriteria: Implement crawl_list_pages() in src/crawler.py that returns list of post metadata dicts
Automatically detect last page number by parsing the '마지막' button's page parameter from the pagination HTML
Iterate from page 1 to last page using URL pattern: https://www.ksae.org/jajak/bbs/?code=J_qna&page={n}
Extract from each row: post number (int), category string, title, author, view count (int), date (YYYY-MM-DD)
Generate detail URL for each post: /jajak/bbs/?number={id}&mode=view&code=J_qna
Apply configurable delay between requests (default 1.5s) to avoid server overload
Save intermediate metadata list to data/raw/post_list.json
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Board URL: https://www.ksae.org/jajak/bbs/?code=J_qna - Table has columns: 번호, 구분, 제목, 등록자, 파일, 조회수, 등록일. Pagination has '마지막' link with page=N param. Currently 11 pages, ~150 posts.

userStories.title: 게시글 상세 페이지 크롤링 및 데이터 저장
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to crawl each post's detail page to extract question body, answer body, and comments, then save all data as structured JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement crawl_detail_page(post_id) in src/crawler.py that returns full post data dict
Extract question title and question body text from detail page
Extract answer body text if answer exists (답변 section)
Extract comments list if comments exist
Record attachment URLs in metadata (do not download files)
Strip HTML tags and normalize whitespace/newlines to clean plain text
Implement retry logic: max 3 attempts per page with exponential backoff
Log failed post IDs to a failures list
Apply configurable delay between requests (default 1.5s)
Save all posts to data/raw/posts.json with schema: {id, category, title, author, date, views, question_body, answer_body, comments[], attachments[], url}
Save crawl metadata to data/raw/crawl_meta.json: {crawled_at, total_count, failed_count, failed_ids[]}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Detail page URL: https://www.ksae.org/jajak/bbs/?number={id}&mode=view&code=J_qna - Posts typically have single answer. Comments managed via JavaScript.

userStories.title: 텍스트 청킹
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to chunk the crawled Q&A data into RAG-optimized segments so that retrieval quality is maximized.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement chunk_posts() in src/chunker.py that reads data/raw/posts.json and outputs chunks
Primary unit: combine question title + question body + answer body into one document per post
If combined text is 512 tokens or fewer, keep as single chunk
If combined text exceeds 512 tokens, split by paragraph/sentence boundaries with 50-token overlap between chunks
Each chunk dict preserves metadata: post_id, category, title, date, url, chunk_index
Save chunks to data/processed/chunks.json as JSON array
Print chunking statistics: total chunks, avg/min/max chunk token length
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Most Q&A posts are short enough to fit in one chunk. Token counting should use a simple tokenizer (e.g. split on whitespace or use tiktoken) for rough estimation.

userStories.title: BGE-M3 임베딩 생성
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to embed all text chunks using the BGE-M3 model to produce dense vectors for vector search.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement embed_chunks() in src/embedder.py that reads data/processed/chunks.json
Load BAAI/bge-m3 model using FlagEmbedding library (BGEM3FlagModel)
Generate dense embedding vectors (1024 dimensions) for each chunk's text
Process in batches of configurable size (default 32)
Automatically use GPU if available (CUDA/MPS), fall back to CPU
Show progress bar using tqdm
Save embeddings to data/processed/embeddings.npy as numpy array with shape (num_chunks, 1024)
Verify embeddings.npy row count matches chunks.json length
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BGE-M3 supports max_length=8192 tokens. Dense output dimension is 1024. FlagEmbedding: from FlagEmbedding import BGEM3FlagModel

userStories.title: Qdrant 벡터 업로드
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to upload embedding vectors and metadata to Qdrant so that vector similarity search is available for the RAG chatbot.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement upload_to_qdrant() in src/uploader.py that reads chunks.json and embeddings.npy
Connect to Qdrant at configurable URL (default http://localhost:6333)
Create collection 'ksae_qna' with vectors config: size=1024, distance=Cosine
If collection already exists, provide recreate option (default: skip creation)
Upload points in batches of 100 with payload: id, category, title, author, date, url, chunk_text, chunk_index
Create keyword payload index on 'category' field for filtered search
Print collection info after upload: total points count, vector dimensions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use qdrant_client.QdrantClient. Categories for filtering: Baja, Formula, EV, 기술, 기타.

userStories.title: CLI 파이프라인 통합
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single CLI entry point that runs the full pipeline (crawl -> chunk -> embed -> upload) and supports running individual stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create main.py with click CLI group
Default command runs full pipeline: crawl -> chunk -> embed -> upload sequentially
Subcommands available: 'crawl', 'chunk', 'embed', 'upload' for running individual stages
Global CLI options: --qdrant-url (default http://localhost:6333), --collection (default ksae_qna), --batch-size (default 32), --delay (default 1.5)
Log start/completion/elapsed time for each pipeline stage
If a stage fails, preserve intermediate results and exit with informative error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use click library for CLI. Each stage reads from the previous stage's output files, so re-running from a specific stage is naturally supported.

userStories.title: 증분 크롤링 지원
userStories.description: As a developer, I need incremental crawling mode so that only new posts are processed without re-crawling the entire board.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --mode option to CLI: 'full' or 'incremental' (default: incremental)
In incremental mode, load existing post IDs from data/raw/posts.json if it exists
Compare board list page IDs against existing IDs, filter to only new post IDs
If no new posts found, print 'No new posts found' and exit gracefully
Crawl only new posts' detail pages
Run new posts through chunk -> embed -> upload pipeline (append to Qdrant, don't recreate collection)
Merge new post data into existing data/raw/posts.json
In full mode, process all posts from scratch (recreate collection if exists)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Incremental mode is the default to avoid unnecessary re-crawling. Full mode is for initial setup or reset.
